msItem.author: Haly ibn Regel
msItem.title: De iudiciis astrorum
msItem.rubric: Hec est figura inicii translationis huius libri die Iovis mensis Marcii 21 die ... era ... Christi 1253 ...
msItem.incipit: In nomine domini hic est liber ... quem Haly Abenragel ... composuit de judiciis astrologie. Quem Yhuda filius Muste [sic]
Dixit Haly filius Abenragel vni deo victorioso

msItem.title: Liber nouem iudicum I 'Celestis circuli'
msItem.note: Here preceded by a table of chapters divided into 12 'domus' (fol. 109) but without prologue.

msItem.author: Japhar
msItem.title: Liber imbrium
msItem.incipit: Superioris discipline inconcussam veritatem prout Judeorum movet auctoritas

listBibl.bibl: pr. 1507 (with works of Alkindi); Carmody, 85–7; Diaz 943; Thorndike/Kibre 1546, 1602

msItem.author: Messahalla
msItem.title: De natiuitatibus
msItem.rubric: Liber Messahale de Nativitatibus, 14 distinctus capitulis Hugonis translacio. Prologus eiusdem ad Michaelem Tirassone antistitem.
msItem.incipit: Libellum hunc Messahale de nativitatibus etsi apud nos Albumazar

listBibl.bibl: ed. C. S. F. Burnett & D. E. Pingree (London 1997); according to them this copy was directly transcribed from the only other manuscript, MS. Digby 159, from St Augustine's Canterbury.

msItem.author: Ahmad ibn al-Muthanna ibn Abd al-Karim [?10th or 11th cent.]
msItem.title: Commentary on tables of Alcoarismi
msItem.rubric: Hamis Benhamie Machumeti frater de geometria mobilis quantitatis et azig, hoc est canonis stellarum rationibus
msItem.note: According to Burnett and Pingree, copied from Cambridge, Gonville and Caius, MS. 456/394, from St Augustine's Canterbury.

listBibl.bibl: El Comentario de ibn al- Mutanna a las tablas astronomicas de al-Jwarizmi ; estudio y edicion critica del texto latino en la version de Hugo Sanctallensis, ed. Eduardo Millás Vendrell (Madrid 1963). "Previously attributed either directly to Hugh of Santalla (Diaz 940) or to Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Biruni as a commentary on al-Fargani on Alcoarismi (Carmody, 155; Thorndike/Kibre 46, 1225; Haskins, Studies in Mediaeval Science, 72–4)" (Sharpe, Identifications)

msItem.author: Guido Bonatti (fl. 1245)
msItem.title: Liber introductorius siue Decem tractatus astronomiae
msItem.incipit: In nomine domini nostri Jhesu Christi misericordie
(fol. 229) Animus est nobilius quod reperitur in homine
msItem.note: See L. Thorndike, History of Magic, pp. 825-40, here in eight treatises each subdivided into parts and chapters. A note on fol. 334v states that this was written by Jacobus Ganton, as was apparently most of the volume.

supportDesc.support: paper (watermark resembles Briquet 7208 according to Burnett and Pingree)

handDesc.handNote: Written in large part by James Ganton (fol. 334v)

binding.p: Red leather on boards, with traces of two clasps, 15th-cent. English work.

origin.origPlace: England, Canterbury (?), Benedictine abbey of St Augustine (?)

history.provenance: Several contents apparently copied directly from manuscripts of St Augustine's Canterbury (see above), but no library markings; 'possibly made at StA for an outside client?' (Barker-Benfield, III, 1805).
There is a table of contents (c. 1500) on fol. i.

A note on the front pastedown 'Nota de durabilitate etc. ordinis fratrum Minorum fo° 308°', and a corresponding note on fol. 308r. Summary Catalogue suggested that the volume belonged to the Franciscan house, Doncaster (by false analogy with MS. Savile 20), rejected by MLGB.

John Dee: fol. 1, 'Joannes Dee 1564' partly obliterated, and autograph note by him (fol. 105); John Dee’s Library Catalogue, ed. R. J. Roberts, and Andrew G. Watson (Bibliographical Society, 1990), M108, pp. 24, 122.
'P. Saunders' (17th cent.): Patrick Saunders (?1582-1638), on whom see Roberts & Watson, pp. 61-63.

recordHist.source:
Description adapted by Matthew Holford (April 2026) from the following sources, with additional reference to published literature as cited.
St Augustine's Abbey, Canterbury, ed. B. C. Barker-Benfield, CBMLC 13 (2008), III, pp. 1804-5 The Liber Aristotilis of Hugo of Santalla, ed. Charles Burnett and David Pingree, Warburg Institute Surveys and Texts, 26 (The Warburg Institute, 1997), pp. 9-10
Summary Catalogue (1937)
Summary Catalogue, Vol. 2 Part 2, p. 1102 Summary Catalogue, Vol. 2 Part 2, p. 1103

listBibl.head: Printed descriptions:
listBibl.bibl: S. J. P. van Dijk, Latin Liturgical Manuscripts in the Bodleian Library, Oxford, vol. 6: Fragments - Office Books, Rituals, Directories (typescript, 1957), p. 217

msItem.title: Breviary
msItem.note: Two leaves from a noted Sarum breviary.

revisionDesc.change: Matthew Holford: description revised to incorporate all information from Summary Catalogue with additional reference to published literature.
Add binding information from Summary Catalogue.
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/jamescummings/Bodleian-msDesc-ODD/blob/master/convertTolkien2Bodley.xsl
